text:
Credibility is not a soft asset.
It is a priced risk factor.
textPreview: Credibility is not a soft asset. | It is a priced risk factor.

text: A data-driven view of how inflation control, policy consistency, forecasting, bonds, and FX combine into institutional trust.

text: One score. Five tests of institutional trust.
textPreview: One score. Five tests of institutional trust.

text: The model deliberately balances mandate delivery, communication quality, forecasting, and market outcomes.
textPreview: The model deliberately balances mandate delivery, communication quality, forecasting, and market outcomes.

text: Inflation anchoring receives the largest weight because price stability is the primary mandate.
textPreview: Inflation anchoring receives the largest weight because price stability is the primary mandate.

text: The design prevents a single market metric from becoming a proxy for credibility.
textPreview: The design prevents a single market metric from becoming a proxy for credibility.

text: Method: weighted sum of five 0–100 sub-indices; weights shown above.
textPreview: Method: weighted sum of five 0–100 sub-indices; weights shown above.

text: Turn the index into a repeatable decision discipline.
textPreview: Turn the index into a repeatable decision discipline.

text: The score is most useful when it changes the questions asked before taking duration, FX, or volatility risk.
textPreview: The score is most useful when it changes the questions asked before taking duration, FX, or volatility risk.

text: Management lesson: credibility should be monitored as a leading operating indicator—not explained after volatility arrives.
textPreview: Management lesson: credibility should be monitored as a leading operating indicator—not explained after volatility arrives.

text: THE EXECUTIVE TAKEAWAY
textPreview: THE EXECUTIVE TAKEAWAY

text:
Credibility is a system property—
and the weakest link gets priced.
textPreview: Credibility is a system property— | and the weakest link gets priced.

textPreview: bp rise in the US 10Y from trough to peak

text: Use multiple pillars; no single outcome is credibility.
textPreview: Use multiple pillars; no single outcome is credibility.

text: Treat communication consistency as a measurable risk factor.
textPreview: Treat communication consistency as a measurable risk factor.

text: Focus on the weakest pillar—it often explains the next repricing.
textPreview: Focus on the weakest pillar—it often explains the next repricing.

text: If credibility moves markets, why leave it qualitative?
